content.full_text:
FPPC
JAN 1

THE CITY OF SANTA CLARA
CALIFORNIA

CITY ATTORNEY
CITY HALL
1500 WARBURTON AVE.
SANTA CLARA, CA 95050
(408) 984-3232
FAX (408) 241-6771

January 3, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 Jay Street, Suite #800
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Sirs:

Re: Legal Opinion on Ability of City Councilperson to Participate
on a Land Acquisition Matter

Factual Background

I am Donald Von Raesfeld, a City Councilperson for the City of
Santa Clara. I was elected to the City Council November 1988.
Prior to my election to the City Council, I had served in the
capacity of City Manager from 3/1/62 through 12/30/86.

I have nine adult children. Currently, one, Myron Von
Raesfeld, is a real estate agent working with Contempo Realty in
their Cupertino office. Myron is married and has two children.

At the present time, Myron and I own a share of a 23-unit
apartment complex at 1020 Elm Street, San Jose, California. Myron
owns approximately a 21% share of the apartment complex. I loaned
him the $40,000 principal at an interest rate of 12.5%. Myron
makes semi-monthly interest-only payments to me. The Principal is
due on sale of the apartments which are now on the market.

The Current Situation

The City of Santa Clara has a plan line which provides for the
widening of Homestead Road between Scott Avenue and Los Padres.
The City has appropriated approximately $1 million dollars in order
to buy the right-of-way and/or property for the widening of
Homestead Road. When possible, the City proposes to purchase 5-
1/2-foot strips along the frontage of properties. There are
approximately 24 properties which have or may be involved either


1 Seven right-of-way strips for street widening have been
dedicated or acquired by purchase.

Fair Political Practice Commission
January 3, 1991
Page 2


as to City acquisition of a frontage strip or a take of the entire
piece of property.

     At one time, a particular piece of property, 2211 Homestead,
owned by Mr. and Mrs. Delmar Warburton of 2211 Homestead Road had
an association with my son Myron.  For a period of time, Myron
represented the Warburton's with respect to the City acquisition of
their entire property.  The advisability of purchasing the entire
property from the Warburton's was discussed by the City Council in
closed session.  I had disqualified myself because of Myron's
involvement, and I was not present at the closed session.

     Myron has withdrawn from representation in any way with the
Warburton's.  He has indicated to me that he will not participate
in the acquisition, if any, the Warburton's property nor in any
other property acquisition by the City in the Homestead widening
area.

     Myron has also indicated to me that he has absolutely no
arrangement with any other agent(s) in the Contempo office to
reciprocate in any way for his withdrawal from participation and/or
representation of any property owners in the Homestead widening
area.  He has assured me that not only will he receive no portion
of any commission received by other agent, if in fact there is a
commission, but that there will be no reciprocity, in any manner,
from other agents for Myron's potentially "lost" commission or
commissions.

Requested Action

     I would respectfully request that the advice attorneys for the
Fair Political Practices Commission give a written opinion as to
whether or not I would be disqualified from participating in
decisions on Homestead property decisions if the non-participation
of my son is as I have represented to you.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [signature]
                                        Donald Von Raesfeld

sections.facts:
I am Donald Von Raesfeld, a City Councilperson for the City of Santa Clara. I was elected to the City Council November 1988. Prior to my election to the City Council, I had served in the capacity of City Manager from 3/1/62 through 12/30/86.

I have nine adult children. Currently, one, Myron Von Raesfeld, is a real estate agent working with Contempo Realty in their Cupertino office. Myron is married and has two children.

At the present time, Myron and I own a share of a 23-unit apartment complex at 1020 Elm Street, San Jose, California. Myron owns approximately a 21% share of the apartment complex. I loaned him the $40,000 principal at an interest rate of 12.5%. Myron makes semi-monthly interest-only payments to me. The Principal is due on sale of the apartments which are now on the market.

The Current Situation

The City of Santa Clara has a plan line which provides for the widening of Homestead Road between Scott Avenue and Los Padres. The City has appropriated approximately $1 million dollars in order to buy the right-of-way and/or property for the widening of Homestead Road. When possible, the City proposes to purchase 5-1/2-foot strips along the frontage of properties. There are approximately 24 properties which have or may be involved either

1 Seven right-of-way strips for street widening have been dedicated or acquired by purchase.

Fair Political Practice Commission  
January 3, 1991  

as to City acquisition of a frontage strip or a take of the entire piece of property.

At one time, a particular piece of property, 2211 Homestead, owned by Mr. and Mrs. Delmar Warburton of 2211 Homestead Road had an association with my son Myron. For a period of time, Myron represented the Warburton's with respect to the City acquisition of their entire property. The advisability of purchasing the entire property from the Warburton's was discussed by the City Council in closed session. I had disqualified myself because of Myron's involvement, and I was not present at the closed session.

Myron has withdrawn from representation in any way with the Warburton's. He has indicated to me that he will not participate in the acquisition, if any, the Warburton's property nor in any other property acquisition by the City in the Homestead widening area.

Myron has also indicated to me that he has absolutely no arrangement with any other agent(s) in the Contempo office to reciprocate in any way for his withdrawal from participation and/or representation of any property owners in the Homestead widening area. He has assured me that not only will he receive no portion of any commission received by other agent, if in fact there is a commission, but that there will be no reciprocity, in any manner, from other agents for Myron's potentially "lost" commission or commissions.

Requested Action

I would respectfully request that the advice attorneys for the Fair Political Practices Commission give a written opinion as to whether or not I would be disqualified from participating in decisions on Homestead property decisions if the non-participation of my son is as I have represented to you.
